description: Figure 7 presents combined EBSD and TEM bright-field analysis of a low-density steel (Fe-Mn-Al-Ti-B system) after tensile straining. Panels (a-a2) display pseudo-3D stereographic EBSD microstructures: (a) an inverse pole figure (IPF) map showing crystallographic orientations along RD, TD, and ND; (a1) a phase map distinguishing austenite (red), δ-ferrite (blue), and TiB2 particles (yellow); and (a2) a kernel average misorientation (KAM) map indicating local strain distribution from low (blue) to high (green) misorientation. Panels (b-b2) show TEM bright-field images of the BCC δ-ferrite phase: (b) a grain interior distant from TiB2 particles with corresponding BCC <110> selected area diffraction pattern (SADP); (b1) extensive dislocation pile-ups and dense tangles at the δ-ferrite/TiB2 interface; and (b2) the corresponding interface SADP (g=0-11). Panels (c-c2) depict the FCC austenite phase: (c) a grain interior away from TiB2 with FCC <001> SADP; (c1) dislocation pile-ups with sparse tangles at the austenite/TiB2 interface; and (c2) the corresponding interface SADP (g=-111). The images reveal significantly higher dislocation accumulation and denser tangles at δ-ferrite/TiB2 interfaces compared to austenite/TiB2 interfaces, supporting the synergistic deformation mechanism where δ-ferrite provides a stable strengthening framework while austenite accommodates plasticity through dislocation activities.
